title.es: Conceptos Estadisticos y Critica a la Volatilidad
title.en: Statistical Concepts and the Volatility Critique

summary_es: Este modulo profundiza en los cuatro momentos estadisticos fundamentales para finanzas: media, varianza (volatilidad), asimetria (skewness) y curtosis. Se demuestra con datos sinteticos que tres distribuciones con volatilidad identica pueden tener riesgos reales drasticamente diferentes cuando difieren en skewness y curtosis. Se explica por que la volatilidad es una medida absurda de riesgo: trata subidas y bajadas como iguales, asume simetria y subestima eventos extremos. Se introduce la no-ergodicidad para mostrar que el valor esperado positivo no garantiza ganancias individuales. Se implementan medidas alternativas de riesgo: VaR, Expected Shortfall, downside deviation y semi-varianza.
summary_en: This module dives deep into the four fundamental statistical moments for finance: mean, variance (volatility), skewness, and kurtosis. Synthetic data demonstrates that three distributions with identical volatility can have drastically different real risks when they differ in skewness and kurtosis. Volatility is shown to be a nonsensical risk measure: it treats gains and losses equally, assumes symmetry, and underestimates extreme events. Non-ergodicity is introduced to show that positive expected value does not guarantee individual gains. Alternative risk measures are implemented: VaR, Expected Shortfall, downside deviation, and semi-variance.
learning_objectives_es: Calcular e interpretar los 4 momentos: media, varianza, skewness, curtosis
Demostrar que distribuciones con misma volatilidad tienen riesgo real diferente
Explicar por que la volatilidad es insuficiente como medida de riesgo
Distinguir valor esperado de experiencia individual (no-ergodicidad)
Implementar VaR, Expected Shortfall y downside deviation en Python
learning_objectives_en: Calculate and interpret the 4 moments: mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis
Demonstrate that distributions with same volatility have different real risk
Explain why volatility is insufficient as a risk measure
Distinguish expected value from individual experience (non-ergodicity)
Implement VaR, Expected Shortfall, and downside deviation in Python
